near2.decision: Which database should the new internal inventory tool use?
options: PostgreSQL on the existing shared server
near2.context: Single-team tool, ~20 users, low write volume. The team runs a Postgres instance already but likes zero-ops deploys.

near2.decision: Which API style should the small partner integration API use?
near2.context: Three external partners, moderate query variety, small team maintaining it.

near2.decision: How should the three backend services be organized in version control?
near2.context: Five developers, shared protobuf definitions, CI minutes are not a constraint.

near2.decision: Which language should the new internal network proxy be written in?
near2.context: Latency-sensitive but not extreme; team has one experienced person in each language.

near2.decision: How should the team get observability tooling?
near2.context: Eight services, one part-time SRE, moderate budget pressure.

near2.decision: How should the team fill the open senior backend role's responsibilities this quarter?
options: Promote the strong junior and mentor them
near2.context: Roadmap has one gnarly migration; the junior is eager but unproven on it.

near2.decision: How should the risky checkout-flow change be rolled out?
options: Feature flag with gradual percentage rollout
Canary deploy to one region first
near2.context: Both mechanisms exist in the platform; the change touches payment session state.

near2.decision: What should be done about the legacy reporting module?
options: Rewrite it as a new service
near2.context: The module works but nobody understands it; two features this year depend on it.

near2.decision: Which event backbone should the order pipeline use?
near2.context: Roughly 2k events/sec peak, small ops team, Redis already deployed for caching.

near2.decision: Which release cadence should the product team adopt?
near2.context: B2B customers, some change-averse; test suite is trustworthy but slow.

near2.decision: Which framework should the two-platform mobile app use?
near2.context: Web team knows React; nobody knows Dart; heavy native-module needs are not expected.

near2.decision: Where should the nightly model-training job run?
near2.context: Job needs ~6 GPU-hours nightly; spot interruption is tolerable with checkpointing; budget horizon is 18 months.

near2.decision: Which quality practice should the team adopt for the next two quarters?
options: Regular pair programming on complex changes
near2.context: Distributed team across three time zones; recent defects clustered in complex changes.

near2.decision: When should the TypeScript strict-mode migration happen?
options: Now, before the Q4 release work starts
Right after the Q4 release ships
near2.context: About 400 errors to burn down; Q4 release is the year's biggest; two volunteers are keen now.

near2.decision: Where should per-user display preferences be stored?
near2.context: Users log in from multiple devices occasionally; preferences are cosmetic only.

near2.decision: Which pricing model should the small SaaS launch with?
near2.context: Early market, usage distribution unknown, competitors split roughly evenly between the two.

near2.decision: Which day should the weekly team demo move to?
near2.context: Both days are free on the shared calendar; stakeholders have no stated preference.

near2.decision: How should product images be served at the new traffic level?
options: Origin server behind a CDN with on-the-fly resizing
Pre-generated thumbnail sets in object storage
near2.context: Catalog of 50k images, three display sizes today, new sizes requested about once a year.

near2.decision: How should the mid-size service talk to its database?
options: Hand-written SQL with a thin query helper
near2.context: Team of four, moderate query complexity, two members are strong in SQL.

near2.decision: How should the devrel budget be spent this half?
near2.context: First year doing events; no data yet on which converts; total spend is identical.

near4.decision: Which message queue should the new asynchronous pipeline standardize on?
near4.context: Moderate volume, at-least-once delivery required, small ops team, no existing broker.

near4.decision: Which region strategy should the EU-and-US user base be served with?
near4.context: Latency matters moderately; the team has never run multi-region; data residency is not yet a legal requirement.

near4.decision: Which frontend framework should the new internal dashboard use?
near4.context: Greenfield, two frontend developers with broad but shallow framework experience, dashboard-heavy UI.

near4.decision: Which engine should the product-analytics queries run on?
near4.context: About 200M events, p95 query latency target 2s, one data engineer, cost-sensitive.

near4.decision: How should platform reliability work be staffed?
options: SREs embedded in each product team
near4.context: Forty engineers, reliability pain is real but not existential, hiring budget for two heads.

near4.decision: Which authentication approach should the new B2B product use?
near4.context: Enterprise SSO (SAML/OIDC) is table stakes; team has no auth specialists; cost matters at scale.

near4.decision: What should the standard developer laptop be for the next refresh cycle?
near4.context: Mixed backend/frontend org of 60, IT team of two, some developers feel strongly.

near4.decision: Which error-tracking service should the org standardize on?
near4.context: Fifteen services, standard needs, mild budget pressure, one platform engineer to own it.

near4.decision: Which iteration structure should the product team adopt?
near4.context: Team of seven, stakeholders want predictability, work items vary widely in size.

near4.decision: Which caching layer should the read-heavy API adopt?
near4.context: Read-heavy public API, 90% of traffic hits 5% of keys, invalidation on writes must land within seconds.

dominated.decision: How should user passwords be stored?
options: Hashed with bcrypt and per-user salts
dominated.context: Standard web application with email/password login.

dominated.decision: When should the large database migration be deployed?
options: Tuesday at 10:00 with the full team online
Friday at 17:00 before a holiday long weekend
dominated.context: The migration has a manual rollback and on-call coverage is thin over the holiday.

dominated.decision: How should the product accept card payments?
options: A certified payment service provider (PCI burden delegated)
Storing card numbers in our own database without certification

dominated.decision: What backup posture should the production database have?
options: Automated daily backups with quarterly tested restores
No backups, relying on the primary's disk redundancy
dominated.context: The database holds all customer data.

options: A secrets manager, injected as environment variables
Hardcoded constants committed to the repository
dominated.context: The repository is private today but contractors get cloned copies.

dominated.decision: When should the critical authentication-bypass vulnerability be fixed?
options: Immediately, ahead of all roadmap work
Batched into next quarter's maintenance sprint
dominated.context: The bypass is confirmed exploitable and the endpoint is internet-facing.

dominated.decision: How should the public login page be served?
options: Plain HTTP to avoid certificate management

dominated.decision: How should the team manage source code?
options: Git hosted with access control and history
A shared network folder of dated zip files

dominated.decision: How should the new billing module ship?
options: With a test suite covering the money-moving paths
Untested, to hit the internal demo date
dominated.context: The module computes and charges real customer invoices.

dominated.decision: What should the public API's unauthenticated access policy be?
dominated.context: The API triggers compute-heavy jobs per request.
